comment: Rivals NEVER attack player territory (Pillar 1). They expand into neutral tiles at expansionRate tiles per game-week. Taunts fire on: rival captures a tile, player fails an attack, player captures a rival tile. Voice: dry, specific, one level of escalation max.

rivals.name: Grillzilla Franchise Group
rivals.defenseStyle: high defense on office tiles, weak on lateNight
rivals.flavour: A 340-store franchise machine. Their burgers are assembled, not cooked. Their margins are spectacular.

onCapture: Grillzilla has opened store #341. The ribbon was cut by a regional manager who has never eaten there.
Another suburb secured. Head office celebrated by reducing patty weight 4 grams.
Grillzilla's expansion continues, per the five-year plan. There is always a five-year plan.
onPlayerFailedAttack: Grillzilla's legal team has noted your attempt. They note everything.
Your campaign was reviewed at the Tuesday standup. It did not make the minutes.
onPlayerCapture: Store #217 has been reclassified as 'underperforming asset'. Head office moves on.
Grillzilla has filed the loss under 'market conditions'. Grillzilla files everything under 'market conditions'.

rivals.name: PATTY CVLT
rivals.defenseStyle: high defense on student and lateNight tiles, weak on families
rivals.flavour: Smash burgers, no menu, no bookings, no vowels. The queue is the marketing.

onCapture: PATTY CVLT has opened in another warehouse. There is no signage. The line is around the block.
New location dropped. They announced it by not announcing it.
PATTY CVLT expands again. The burger is $26 and comes with a philosophy.
onPlayerFailedAttack: PATTY CVLT's regulars noticed your flyers. One of them wrote a zine about it.
Your push was discussed on their Discord. The verdict: 'mid'.
onPlayerCapture: PATTY CVLT has left the suburb, calling it 'over'. They were planning to leave anyway. They insist.
The warehouse is yours. The philosophy has relocated.
